title: Check if a number is even or odd
description: Write a Bash script that reads a number from stdin and prints 'even' if the number is even, or 'odd' if it is odd.
script:
#!/bin/bash
read n
if (( n % 2 == 0 )); then
  echo "even"
else
  echo "odd"
fi
answers: if (( n % 2 == 0 )); then echo "even"; else echo "odd"; fi
if [ $((n % 2)) -eq 0 ]; then echo "even"; else echo "odd"; fi
if (( n % 3 == 0 )); then echo "even"; else echo "odd"; fi
while read n; do echo $n; done
hint: Use the modulo operator % to check divisibility by 2.

title: What does this script output?
description: Given the Bash script below, select ALL lines that will be printed to stdout when it is executed.
script:
#!/bin/bash
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  if (( i % 2 == 0 )); then
    echo "even: $i"
  fi
done
hint: The script only prints numbers where i % 2 == 0. Which values of i satisfy that?

title: Variable scope and arithmetic
description: What is the final output of this script? Select the correct printed lines in order.

title: String operations in Bash
description: Select all statements that are TRUE about the script below.
answers: ${#str} prints the length of the string
hint: In Bash: ${#var} = length, ${var:offset:len} = substring, ${var,,} = lowercase, ${var^^} = uppercase.

title: What does this function script output?
description: Analyze the script carefully and select the exact lines printed to stdout, in the correct order.
hint: The function is called twice with different arguments, then 'Done' is echoed. local variables don't leak outside.
